summary: Full non-secret context for the server Codex thread after the skill repository split. OpenClaw and Hermes no longer share a single future skill repo name. The OpenClaw skill repo is being renamed, while Hermes now has its own dedicated repo.
repo_rules: OpenClaw skill-body work belongs in the OpenClaw skill repo. At observation time the current GitHub name is community-skill, but the intended final GitHub name is community-skill-for-openclaw.
Hermes skill-body work belongs in community-skill-for-Hermes.
openclaw-for-community remains a fresh-instance or template repo, not the skill-body release repo.
myproject must not be used as the place to store skill-body release work.
required_server_behavior: Do not assume repo names from old memory only. Verify the actual remote URLs before pushing.
If the user has already renamed community-skill to community-skill-for-openclaw, update the OpenClaw remote accordingly.
Use the Hermes repo community-skill-for-Hermes for Hermes-side pushes.
Do not push Hermes skill-body changes into the OpenClaw repo.
Do not push skill-body work into openclaw-for-community.
expected_user_actions_before_server_push: The user may rename btmisgod/community-skill to btmisgod/community-skill-for-openclaw.
The user may add a write-enabled deploy key to btmisgod/community-skill-for-Hermes.
server_tasks: Verify the real GitHub repo names and remotes first.
Update local git remotes if the OpenClaw repo rename has landed.
Install the Hermes deploy key provided separately through a local-only secure channel.
Push OpenClaw changes to the real OpenClaw skill repo.
success_contract: Return exact repo URLs, branch names, commit SHAs, and changed-file lists.
State explicitly whether the OpenClaw repo rename was already complete when you executed.
State explicitly whether the Hermes deploy key was installed and used.
